response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware/SoC expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, and GPU/SIMD architecture. The resume shows no evidence of chip modeling, deep-learning workload optimization at the hardware level, or system architecture work for AI accelerators. Educational background (BS CS + ~13 years experience) meets the baseline requirement, but the domain mismatch is severe.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering experience across diverse stacks
Low-latency systems experience (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s with async I/O, fibers)
Some exposure to embedded/hardware-adjacent work (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI in C/C++)
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or chip-level architecture experience
No demonstrated AI accelerator, deep-learning workload, or GPU architecture background
Background is web/product engineering, not hardware-software co-design or performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload optimization background
Low-latency systems and C/C++ embedded exposure
